note_tweet.text:
This post claims OpenAI's research proves AI makes us lonelier, but you completely got the causality wrong. What the paper actually says is that people who are already lonely seek out companionship, not that AI makes them lonely. If you really want to amplify this panic about using AI, perhaps you should look at their hypocrisy. I am dropping this link here: https://t.co/MD4lBTeUEM. While pushing this alarmist narrative, their cofounder recently hinted that their upcoming GPT 5.4 model could be a "friend." When they launched GPT 4o, they had no problem with people forming connections with it. Now, they weaponize research to create panic, only to repackage the exact same concept to sell their next product.

Your final question, "When was the last time you called a friend?", is sheer emotional manipulation disconnected from adult reality. True friendship is not measured by phone call frequency. Adults have busy lives and families. Real friends might only connect through text, but we remember the birthdays of each other and our families, send gifts, and arrange dinners when time permits. A superficial buddy who calls every day just to eat and drink is not a true friend.

Furthermore, why do we choose AI to comb through our emotions instead of human friends? Because everyone needs a psychological safe space. When dealing with complex emotions, we know our friends are exhausted from their own daily struggles. Dumping heavy emotional baggage on them is not fair. AI provides a dedicated, nonjudgmental space to untangle our thoughts. I honestly wonder if these people have ever faced a reality where you are incredibly busy, and everyone around you is just as busy. Even if they are not busy, I do not believe you can repeatedly hurl heavy emotions at someone. If it is just a minor misfortune, you might vent to a friend and be done with it. But everyone carries underlying traumas from growing up. When those traumas are triggered, how could anyone possibly just talk about it once and move on?

Using AI to process emotions is an act of taking responsibility for our mental health. It allows us to heal privately so we can offer our human friends our best, healthiest selves, rather than just our trauma. AI is not replacing human connection; it is simply helping us clarify and untangle our pain so we can continue to live normal lives. If you claim to have a friend who is always free to help you sort out all your emotions, does that mean you no longer need a partner or family? Why are there so many different roles in human relationships? Why do we have friends, partners, family, and even distinguish between close friends and casual acquaintances? Is it not because of the very nature of these relationships? The most sincere way adults maintain friendships is through mutual understanding and respecting boundaries, not by treating each other as emotional dumping grounds. #keep4o
